title: Electrical service and maintenance mechanics and repairers
description: Research, design, develop, or test computer or computer-related equipment for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. May supervise the manufacturing and installation of computer or computer-related equipment and components. Research, design, develop, or test electronic components and systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use employing knowledge of electronic theory and materials properties. Design electronic circuits and components for use in fields such as telecommunications, aerospace guidance and propulsion control, acoustics, or instruments and controls. Design and implement radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems used to track shipments or goods. (Based on 33 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct or train workers to install, maintain, or repair electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures; inspect and test electrical systems, equipment, or components to identify hazards, defects, or need for repair, and ensure compliance with codes.

Install ground leads and connect power cables to electrical equipment, such as motors; plan layout and installation of electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures based on job specifications and local codes.

Advise management on the safety of continued operation of equipment, perform testing of electrical systems or continuity of circuits using testing devices to ensure compatibility and safety, and maintain current electrician's license to meet regulations.

Prepare sketches or follow blueprints to determine the location of wiring or equipment and ensure conformance to safety codes, using tools such as power tools, measuring devices, and testing equipment.

Assemble, install, test, or maintain electrical or electronic wiring, equipment, appliances, apparatus, or fixtures with hand or power tools; repair or replace wiring, equipment, or fixtures as needed.
Work from ladders, scaffolds, or roofs to install, maintain, or repair electrical systems; place conduit, pipes, or tubing within designated areas and pull insulated wires or cables through conduits.
Perform physically demanding tasks, such as digging trenches for conduit placement and moving or lifting heavy objects; adhere to safety practices and procedures during work.

Diagnose malfunctioning systems or components using test equipment and hand tools to locate and repair problems; conduct testing of electrical connections to ensure power supplies and communications links are functioning properly.
Install and maintain electrical distribution and transmission systems, including conduits, cables, and related equipment such as transformers and circuit breakers.
Inspect and test power lines and auxiliary equipment to identify and locate issues, ensuring proper functioning and safety assessment using testing instruments.
Drive vehicles to transport equipment to job sites and communicate with bases using telephones or two-way radios.
Conduct inspections of equipment on a regular basis and maintain records pertaining to facilities and equipment; complete reports on project status or other details using computer software.
Install, maintain, and repair electronic equipment or instruments such as televisions or radios; demonstrate equipment usage to customers and respond to inquiries.
Collaborate with other workers and engineers to locate and correct malfunctions and coordinate work assignments.
Climb poles, use truck-mounted booms, and enter confined spaces for installation, maintenance, or inspection of systems and equipment.
Clean and maintain tools, test equipment, and motor vehicles to ensure proper operation.
Test and troubleshoot electronic components and systems using circuit testers, oscilloscopes, and voltmeters to ensure accurate diagnostics and repairs.
Join cables using splicing techniques, and cut and peel lead sheathing prior to splicing to ensure quality connections and operational integrity.
Transport cables and wires between components, ensuring proper installation and connection to electrical systems or communication links.
Coordinate installation procedures for underground grounding systems and measure distances for precise equipment locations.
Assemble components such as switches, electrical controls, and junction boxes, using hand tools or soldering irons.
Adjust, repair, or replace malfunctioning components or assemblies, using hand tools or soldering irons.
Keep records of maintenance and repair work performed on appliances, documenting repairs required, parts used, and labor time.
Connect components to assemblies such as radio systems, instruments, or in-flight refueling systems, using hand tools and soldering irons.
Install electrical and electronic components, assemblies, and systems in various applications, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering irons.
Inspect, maintain, and replace batteries as needed, and monitor and modify the functioning of equipment to ensure compliant operation.
Test and verify hardware and support peripherals to ensure compliance with specifications and requirements, by recording and analyzing test data.
Repair and rebuild defective mechanical parts in electric motors, generators, and related equipment, using hand and power tools.
Analyze user needs and recommend appropriate hardware, and select materials assuring compliance with specifications and product requirements.
Provide training and support to system designers and users, and provide technical support to designers, marketing, and engineering teams during product development.
Evaluate hardware configurations based on reporting formats required, cost constraints, and security restrictions.
Conduct inspections of equipment to locate damage or worn parts and diagnose malfunctions, following work orders or schematic drawings.
Disassemble defective equipment and appliances so that repairs can be made, using hand tools, and reassemble units after repairs, making necessary adjustments.
Replace worn and defective parts such as switches, bearings, or defective wiring in a variety of equipment and devices.
Lubricate moving parts and maintain them, cleaning or inspecting mechanical components such as door closers or motors.
Study blueprints and schematic diagrams to determine methods of installing or repairing electrical systems, including security systems and electric door openers.
Keep informed of new products and developments within the industry to update knowledge and skills.
Operate lifts, winches or other lifting equipment as required for moving heavy equipment or units during repair and installations.
Trace electrical circuits following diagrams, and conduct tests to locate faults within systems or components while testing and repairing as necessary.
Assess risks and determine customer security requirements during consultations, and provide appropriate advice and solutions for installations.
Fabricate parts and test aids as required.
Assemble and modify existing pieces of equipment to meet special needs, and make adjustments to systems or mechanisms where necessary.
Disassemble appliances and mechanical devices such as valves for diagnosis and repairs, employing hand tools and equipment.
Record maintenance information, including test results and repairs made, to maintain accurate service documentation.
Set appliance thermostats, and check to ensure they are functioning properly.

Inspect and test operation and components of gate crossings, signals, signal equipment, and various railroad service equipment, including gas pipelines and associated equipment, to locate leaks and faulty connections, and determine pressure and flow of gas. Additionally, respond to emergency calls for issues such as gas leaks.

Take measurements to determine appliance fit in installation locations, performing any minor carpentry or installation work needed, including installing gas pipes and water lines to connect appliances to existing gas lines or plumbing.
Test and examine appliances, including adjustment of pilot lights on gas stoves, and inspect mechanical parts, circuitry, and connections, making initial installation adjustments as necessary.
Disconnect and reconnect electrical wiring for appliances, including connecting hoses and checking electrical installations for proper wiring and grounding.
Clean, lubricate, and repair appliances; provide repair estimates; and recommend whether to repair or replace appliances based on inspection findings.
Respond to customer inquiries regarding appliance operation, provide instruction on maintenance, and keep records of repairs and maintenance tasks performed.
Participate in ongoing training to maintain skillsets current with new technologies in appliance repair and installations.

Identify installation locations for photovoltaic (PV) systems, conduct routine maintenance, and compile or maintain records of system operation and performance for photovoltaic systems, ensuring compliance with standards.

Connect and test radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems, including supporting software, by integrating components and monitoring their performance. Documenting details of RFID technology.
Evaluate project requirements to inform the installation and configuration of electrical installations, electrical equipment components, and systems, ensuring technical adequacy and compatibility.
Provide technical support or instructions concerning electronics equipment standards and assist in the repair of electrical or electronic systems using various tools and diagnostic instruments.
Diagnose and repair issues affecting complex electronics, perform tests using diagnostic instruments, and maintain inventory of parts needed for ongoing service and repair tasks.
Analyze equipment performance records and recorded test data to locate malfunctions or evaluate modifications, ensuring optimum equipment functioning and efficiency.
Install new equipment, including electrical and electronic components, and configure necessary software or peripherals, maintaining parts inventories for efficient operation.
Maintain records of equipment maintenance work or repairs, including performance problems, repairs, calibrations, or tests.
Examine work orders and converse with equipment operators to detect equipment problems and ascertain whether mechanical or human errors contributed to the problems.
Operate machines to test the functioning of parts or mechanisms and demonstrate equipment functions and features to machine operators.
Travel to customers' stores or offices to service machines or provide emergency repair service.
Reinstall software programs or adjust settings on existing software to fix machine malfunctions, and test electrical components and wiring using test meters, soldering equipment, and hand tools.
Advise customers concerning equipment operation, maintenance, programming, and management regarding customer satisfaction or product performance.

Run signal quality and connectivity tests for individual cables, and record results; test faulty equipment to diagnose malfunctions using test equipment or software, applying knowledge of functional operation of electronic units and systems.

Inspect components of industrial equipment for accurate assembly and installation or defects, such as loose connections or frayed wires; inspect electronic equipment, instruments, products, or systems to ensure conformance to specifications and safety standards.
Clean, oil, or adjust mechanical parts to maintain machines' operating efficiency and prevent breakdowns; clean machines and machine parts using cleaning solvents, cloths, and other equipment.
Consult with customers, supervisors, or engineers to plan the layout of equipment and resolve problems in system operation or maintenance.
Maintain inventory of spare parts and requisition parts as necessary; research, order, and maintain parts inventory for services and repairs.
Coordinate efforts with other workers involved in installing or maintaining equipment or components.

Study blueprints, schematics, manuals, or other specifications to determine installation procedures; prepare necessary criteria and procedures for successful conduct of projects regarding site preparation or installation.

Calibrate testing instruments and installed or repaired equipment to prescribed specifications; determine feasibility of using standardized equipment or develop specifications for necessary equipment.
Repair or adjust equipment, machines, or defective components, replacing worn parts; repair or replace defective or worn parts such as gaskets or seals in watertight electrical equipment.
Test units for operability before and after repairs.

Install repaired equipment in various settings, such as industrial or military establishments; assemble machines according to specifications using hand or power tools. Assemble and install equipment using hand tools and power tools.
Dismantle machines to examine parts for wear or defects, using hand or power tools, and reassemble equipment after completion of inspections, testing, or repairs.
Diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs, using diagnostic equipment, and operate and inspect machines or heavy equipment to diagnose defects.
Direct or coordinate activities concerned with manufacture, construction, installation, maintenance, or modification of electronic equipment, products, or systems.
Collaborate with other workers to repair or move machines, machine parts, or equipment; assist in the assembly of wind generators or the construction of wind farms.
Perform routine maintenance on machinery and equipment, including cleaning, lubricating, or adjusting parts.
Transport machine parts, tools, and equipment between work areas using cranes or hoists.
Inspect components such as bearings, seals, gaskets, and coupler assemblies to determine if repairs are needed.
Identify defects and assess the extent of wear and damage of equipment.
Maintain tool and spare parts inventories required for repair, installation, or replacement services.
Record details of repairs made and parts used; record production, repair, and machine maintenance information.
Analyze test results, machine error messages, or information obtained from operators to diagnose equipment problems.
Examine and listen to equipment, read inspection reports, and confer with customers to locate and diagnose malfunctions.
Adjust, maintain, and repair or replace subassemblies, using hand tools, jacks, and cranes; troubleshoot equipment, electrical components, hydraulics, or other mechanical systems.
Test mechanical products and equipment after repair or assembly to ensure proper performance and compliance with specifications; start machines and observe mechanical operation to detect problems.
Update and maintain operational reports or charts.
Overhaul and test machines or equipment to ensure operating efficiency.
Drive trucks to haul tools and equipment for on-site repair of large machinery.
Perform maintenance on agricultural machinery and vehicles, such as tractors and harvesters.
Install and repair agricultural irrigation, plumbing, and sprinkler systems.
Repair bent or torn sheet metal and repair car upholstery as needed.
Adjust repaired or replaced units as needed to ensure proper operation.
Perform scheduled maintenance, clean units and components, and maintain equipment service records.
Repair, fabricate, and install fixtures and fittings such as steel or wood components and electrical equipment, using blueprints, shop sketches, and instruction manuals.
Disassemble units such as water pumps, control valves, and compressors for repairs.
Replace defective wiring and insulation, tighten electrical connections, and adjust, repair, or replace defective wiring and relays in various systems using tools.
Inspect and test electrical systems and equipment to locate and diagnose malfunctions, using visual inspections and testing devices.
Install new fuses, electrical cables, or power sources, and reassemble and test equipment after repairs.
Confer with customers and personnel to determine the nature of malfunctions and coordinate work activities.
Inspect work areas and tools, provide safety training, and monitor tool and part inventories to ensure working conditions.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures, and compile operational or personnel records as needed.
Measure, cut, and install frameworks and conduit to support and connect wiring, control panels, and junction boxes, using hand tools.
Estimate costs of repairs based on parts and labor requirements and participate in budget preparation.
Monitor employees' work levels and performance, and recommend or initiate personnel actions as necessary.
Conduct or arrange for training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques, and investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, or job orders to assist in the repair or maintenance work.
Measure, mix, prepare, and test chemical solutions for machinery repair or cleaning (Removed: 7).
Install and repair flooring, fixtures, walls, plumbing, steps, and platforms (Removed: 10).
Repair window sash frames, attach weather stripping, or replace window glass (Removed: 14).
Monitor and evaluate employee safety (Removed: 40).
Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work (Removed: 48).

emerging_tasks.task: Set up service for customers, installing, connecting, testing, or adjusting equipment, such as digital subscriber line modems.

Inventory and store parts, tools, and equipment.
